verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the β-subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase Complex V (F1 sector). The protein is a soluble component of the F1 catalytic core, assembled on the inner mitochondrial membrane facing the matrix. It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no structural role in membrane integration. The HGNC gene-group lineage confirms mitochondrial complex V membership. No documented cancer ecto-pool, cell-death externalization, developmental germline display, or activation-induced surface translocation applies to this core housekeeping enzyme. No dual PM/mitochondrial localization or lysosomal exocytosis pathway is reported. The protein does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane.
